hypothesis: A causally available prior-session option-implied variance-risk-premium state can select among a symmetric futures breakout, a symmetric extension fade, and abstention for the next ES/NQ session, producing positive executable stressed utility without transferring a future direction.
why_target_velocity_can_improve: The option surface measures a forward-looking volatility price. Conditioning a finite direction-neutral execution lattice on implied-versus-realized volatility may avoid paying breakout costs in overpriced-volatility states and avoid fading genuinely expanding states.
strongest_argument_against: Only twenty listed sessions are available, the preceding settlement-surface directional veto added no information, option inputs are not directly available in the Topstep deployment plane, and a futures strategy may not capture an option variance-risk premium after costs.
smallest_decisive_experiment: Use the existing sealed forty ES/NQ surfaces and underlying one-minute bars. Fit one discovery-only regularized action classifier over BREAKOUT_OCO, FADE_EXTENSION and ABSTAIN; freeze it before opening four validation and four final-development sessions; require positive held-role stressed utility and uplift versus the best frozen static action, action flip and deterministic random control.
distinctness_reason: The state is exogenous prior-session option-implied volatility relative to trailing realized volatility and it selects a direction-neutral execution geometry. It is not an endogenous variance clock, a directional transfer, a futures-curve spread, or a veto over the prior structural anchors.
next_materially_distinct_alternative: Close the option-implied representation after failure and move to a non-option role-conditioned session-path analogue or another existing-data representation; do not tune neighbouring VRP thresholds.
